Video.title: Video
Video.description: Represents a video entity for the CineView application.

url.description: The URL of the video (e.g., YouTube iframe src).

definition.description: Stores all user-specific data, including profile and video information. Only the authenticated user can read or write their own documents and subcollections.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed for user-specific data isolation. Each user's data is stored in a subcollection under their unique user ID. The main `/users/{userId}` path stores profile information, and a nested `/users/{userId}/video/{videoId}` collection stores that user's video data. This structure ensures that a user can only access their own data, which is enforced by Firestore security rules. Anonymous authentication is used to provide a unique `userId` to every visitor without requiring them to sign up. This aligns with the requirement for user-specific, isolated video content.
